summary: Gruber announces that Daring Fireball has joined The Deck, a boutique ad network for web and design professionals alongside A List Apart, Coudal Partners, and Signal vs. Noise. He explains that The Deck's advertising philosophy closely mirrors his own homegrown sponsorship system, with the key difference being the addition of small graphics alongside text. The shared principles include one ad per page, advertiser vetting, limited spots with equal rotation, and no animation. Gruber credits his friend Jim Coudal as the mastermind behind The Deck and notes their collaborative history in shaping these advertising ideals. He closes by thanking the companies that supported Daring Fireball through his previous sponsorship system, with special mention to Jewelboxing for continuous support from day one.
key_points: The Deck operates on principles contrary to conventional web advertising: one ad per page, vetted advertisers, limited spots, and no animation
Gruber's advertising philosophy was developed in collaboration with Jim Coudal, making The Deck a natural evolution of his homegrown system
The approach is framed as superior for both readers (no distraction or insult) and advertisers (no competition for attention on the same page)
The transition represents Daring Fireball joining an exclusive network of respected web and design publications
money_quotes: Each one of these principles is contrary to the conventional wisdom in web advertising — most sites that draw revenue from advertising attempt to cram as many ads as possible on each page, in as big and garish a format as possible.

Yet I feel strongly that our policies make for a superior experience both for you, the reader, who is neither distracted by animation nor insulted by "punch the monkey" nonsense, and for the sponsors, who do not have to compete for attention from other advertisers on the same page.
Advertisers are limited to products and services we (the member sites of The Deck) have paid for or use.
That The Deck's policies are so similar to my own is not a coincidence.
key_insight: Restraint in web advertising — fewer ads, vetted sponsors, no animation — serves both readers and advertisers better than the conventional approach of maximizing ad density and visual noise.
